title: The Saga Of Carl

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Opening of episode
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase, perfectly integrated but routine at this point

jokes.text: To me, it's legs versus boobs.
jokes.setup: Announcer describing tennis match between two female players
jokes.punchline: Homer reduces tennis to body parts
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer objectification humor, well-executed but predictable

jokes.text: But I'm watching soft-core tennis.
jokes.setup: Family wants to change channel
jokes.punchline: Homer describes women's tennis as 'soft-core'
jokes.explanation: Clever phrase that captures Homer's perspective perfectly

jokes.text: Are you gonna walk upstairs and ask her?
jokes.setup: Homer questions if Marge really said kids could watch their show
jokes.punchline: Bart knows Homer's laziness will prevent fact-checking
jokes.explanation: Sharp character moment showing Bart manipulating Homer's laziness

jokes.text: No.
jokes.setup: Bart's challenge about checking with Marge
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate, honest admission of laziness
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - single word captures Homer's character completely

jokes.text: From a Shaolin monastery in darkest Transylvania come helpful hell-spawn. With homicidal honor, they rampage for peace.
jokes.setup: TV show description
jokes.punchline: Contradictory phrases: 'helpful hell-spawn,' 'homicidal honor,' 'rampage for peace'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant oxymoronic phrasing that satirizes kids' action shows

jokes.text: Planet power!
jokes.setup: Homer defending the old kids show
jokes.punchline: Homer enthusiastically shouting a baby show catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Homer's manchild nature on full display

jokes.text: Kung Fu Werewolf chop! Blob Jitsu kick!
jokes.setup: Kids imitating their new show
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous martial arts names mixing monsters and techniques
jokes.explanation: Solid parody of anime/martial arts naming conventions

jokes.text: Samurai shampoo blast! Ninja throwing soap!
jokes.setup: Kids continue martial arts play in bathroom
jokes.punchline: Incorporating bathroom items into martial arts moves
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation that incorporates the setting into the absurd martial arts theme

jokes.text: Did this really happen? Pill attack! Actual sword attack!
jokes.setup: Grampa's confusion about reality vs TV show
jokes.punchline: Kids escalating from pills to actual weapons on confused elderly man
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Grampa's confusion and kids' obliviousness to danger

jokes.text: Damn it.
jokes.setup: Homer threatens educational trip to science museum
jokes.punchline: Kids' resigned curse word reaction
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction showing kids' disdain for education

jokes.text: You did it, baby. You promised no fun, and you delivered.
jokes.setup: At boring science museum
jokes.punchline: Marge sarcastically congratulating Homer for making good on anti-fun promise
jokes.explanation: Sharp sarcasm that plays to Marge's wit and Homer's incompetence

jokes.text: What are the odds of meeting you here?
jokes.setup: Pascal introduces himself as inventor of probability theory
jokes.punchline: Pascal asking about odds - his own area of expertise
jokes.explanation: Clever self-referential joke that works on educational and comedic levels

jokes.text: Yeah, I always go with three, the number of brothers and sisters I, uh, Hunger Games-ed in the womb.
jokes.setup: Carl explaining his lottery number choice
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to eliminating siblings in utero using pop culture reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark humor that uses modern reference to describe biological reality

jokes.text: And I'm 69. Because people always laugh when you say '69.' No one knows why.
jokes.setup: Barney explaining his lottery number
jokes.punchline: Meta-joke about not understanding why 69 is funny while being funny
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary that makes the obvious joke while commenting on it

jokes.text: Oh, no. That's just gasoline and hot dog water.
jokes.setup: Lenny suggests they're already at a bar for drinks
jokes.punchline: Moe reveals his bar only serves industrial fluids
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe moment - disgusting bar conditions delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: So freaking far in the ground, baby.
jokes.setup: Homer asks if pool will be in-ground
jokes.punchline: Lenny's enthusiastic over-the-top response about depth
jokes.explanation: Lenny's excitement building beyond reasonable levels

jokes.text: I'm glad we're the kind of people who are worried about Carl's safety instead of thinking that he ripped us off.
jokes.setup: Carl hasn't returned with lottery ticket
jokes.punchline: Homer saying exactly what they're all thinking while denying it
jokes.explanation: Classic ironic statement that reveals true thoughts while denying them

jokes.text: The power of friendship is so amazing, I don't feel at all suspicious of Carl.
jokes.setup: Continuing to deny suspicion of Carl
jokes.punchline: Homer over-protesting his trust in increasingly obvious way
jokes.explanation: Escalates the denial pattern, making suspicion more obvious

jokes.text: ♪ Four best friends ♪ Who never quarrel ♪ Homer Simpson, Lenny, Moe and... ♪ And... And no one.
jokes.setup: Moe tries to sing their friendship song
jokes.punchline: Song breaks down when he can't include Carl anymore
jokes.explanation: Effective awkward moment as reality breaks their established routine

jokes.text: If I ever see that Carl again, I'm gonna freeze him, chop him up into ice cubes and scoop him into the urinal trough at the Gathering of the Juggalos!
jokes.setup: Moe expressing anger at Carl
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific, elaborate revenge involving pop culture reference
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific and disgusting threat that perfectly fits Moe's character

jokes.text: See, I bet he flew to Quebec to bring us back authentic French-Canadian sugar pie.
jokes.setup: Everyone assumes Carl stole the money
jokes.punchline: Lenny's innocent, optimistic explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lenny naivety - oddly specific innocent explanation

jokes.text: I was gonna be one of those guys who's really into hot sauce, you know? Hot sauce suspenders, hot sauce pajamas. Bolo tie with a little chili pepper on it.
jokes.setup: Lenny explaining his plans for lottery money
jokes.punchline: Elaborate hot sauce lifestyle with specific accessories
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific identity that Lenny had planned, perfectly in character

jokes.text: We talk about guy stuff. Like baseball and trucks and when guys lift too much weight and their bungus drops out.
jokes.setup: Homer defending that men don't discuss personal topics
jokes.punchline: List escalates to bizarre made-up anatomical disaster
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from normal guy topics to absurd medical condition

jokes.text: Ice-land? Is that even a real place? I thought it was Superman's Superman cave.
jokes.setup: Learning Carl is from Iceland
jokes.punchline: Homer confusing Iceland with Fortress of Solitude
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer ignorance mixing real geography with comic book knowledge

jokes.text: I guess I'm only bringing one guitar on this revenge trip.
jokes.setup: Airline baggage fees
jokes.punchline: Lenny apparently travels with multiple guitars normally
jokes.explanation: Unexpected character detail that suggests Lenny's hidden musical aspirations

jokes.text: We're broken. We're pretty broken.
jokes.setup: Marge says family isn't broken
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact correction and emphasis
jokes.explanation: Homer's blunt honesty about family dysfunction

jokes.text: Visual gag: Marge sleeps with a body pillow shaped like Homer to replace his pushing
jokes.setup: Homer won't be there to push Marge to edge of bed
jokes.punchline: Marge has prepared a Homer-shaped pillow substitute
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual joke that perfectly captures their relationship dynamics

jokes.text: It says here Iceland is green, and Greenland is icy. The Vikings switched the names to trick everybody.
jokes.setup: Lenny reading about Iceland
jokes.punchline: Historical fact about misleading names
jokes.explanation: Common knowledge presented as revelation, mildly amusing

jokes.text: Hey, Vikings, it's still pretty damn cold! I only packed shorts.
jokes.setup: Learning about Viking naming trick
jokes.punchline: Homer addressing ancient Vikings about current weather problem
jokes.explanation: Homer's illogical blame assignment across centuries

jokes.text: You say he's black!
jokes.setup: Homer describing Carl awkwardly without mentioning race
jokes.punchline: Moe forcing Homer to be direct while being uncomfortable himself
jokes.explanation: Awkward racial dynamic played for uncomfortable humor

jokes.text: Ingimar Ogmundsson, how progresses your gonorrhea?
jokes.setup: Explanation that Iceland is small, judgmental, and nosy
jokes.punchline: Immediate demonstration with personal medical inquiry
jokes.explanation: Perfect demonstration of the concept just explained, with embarrassing specificity

jokes.text: It heals, but slowly.
jokes.setup: Personal medical question asked publicly
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact response to inappropriate question
jokes.explanation: Deadpan acceptance of embarrassing public medical discussion

jokes.text: burning our sweaters and feasting upon our tiny horses
jokes.setup: Description of barbarian invasion damage
jokes.punchline: List of distinctly Icelandic losses
jokes.explanation: Culturally specific details that make invasion personally Icelandic

jokes.text: Apparently, screwing over your friends is in Carl's blood-- his adopted blood.
jokes.setup: Learning about Carl's family history
jokes.punchline: Homer's confusion about how adopted traits work
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic failure about genetics vs. adoption

jokes.text: and that's why his sweatpants say 'Hjorleifsstrati' across the butt
jokes.setup: Learning Carl's address explains his lottery number
jokes.punchline: Retroactive explanation for Carl's clothing detail
jokes.explanation: Ties together earlier visual detail with current revelation

jokes.text: All drivers must be over the age of 25... and check. Love you, Mom.
jokes.setup: Lenny reading rental car contract requirements
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's calling his mother to confirm age requirement
jokes.explanation: Unexpected revelation that Lenny needs parental confirmation of basic facts

jokes.text: It's no use. We're perfectly matched.
jokes.setup: Chase scene between Carl and his friends
jokes.punchline: Carl declaring equality during what's presumably slapstick
jokes.explanation: Standard chase scene comment, likely funnier visually

jokes.text: Give us some answers, or you'll get a mouthful of rotten shark fermented in its own urine.
jokes.setup: Homer threatening Carl for answers
jokes.punchline: Threat using disgusting Icelandic delicacy
jokes.explanation: Culturally appropriate threat using genuinely disgusting local food

jokes.text: No, no, anything but the inedible, repulsive food of my native land.
jokes.setup: Homer's threat with Icelandic food
jokes.punchline: Carl insulting his own country's cuisine while begging
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating humor about his homeland's cuisine

jokes.text: we're not friends. Friends share their feelings, their hopes, their dreams. Friends know their friends are from Iceland! We are just guys who sit next to each other at a bar
jokes.setup: Carl explaining why he didn't tell them about taking money
jokes.punchline: Harsh but accurate assessment of their shallow relationship
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest observation about male friendship that cuts deep

jokes.text: When the nice ones snap, it's always a good show.
jokes.setup: Lenny becoming violent
jokes.punchline: Moe's detached commentary on character types
jokes.explanation: Moe's cynical wisdom about human nature delivered perfectly

jokes.text: It's World War II all over again. America kicks Iceland's ass.
jokes.setup: Taking Carl's saga page
jokes.punchline: Homer framing minor conflict as historical warfare
jokes.explanation: Homer's tendency to inflate minor conflicts to historical proportions

jokes.text: I say we make it into saga soup and have ourselves a soup sip.
jokes.setup: Homer suggesting they destroy Carl's saga page
jokes.punchline: Alliterative threat with made-up phrase 'soup sip'
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that captures Homer's destructive impulses

jokes.text: my doctor says it's better for me not to sleep
jokes.setup: Moe having glasses to help them read ancient Icelandic
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of medical advice against sleep
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Moe-esque casual reference to severe medical issue

jokes.text: The Carlsons then wrote down this saga so no one would ever forget their treachery and cowardice.
jokes.setup: Reading about Carl's family's crimes
jokes.punchline: The criminals documented their own crimes for posterity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - criminals creating permanent record of their shame

jokes.text: They were the original ultimate scumbags.
jokes.setup: Learning how bad Carl's ancestors really were
jokes.punchline: Homer's simple summary of complex historical treachery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer simplification of complex historical narrative

jokes.text: You have endured barbarian invasions, total financial collapse and a lesbian prime minister.
jokes.setup: Homer addressing Iceland about their hardships
jokes.punchline: Listing actual hardships ending with irrelevant detail about PM's sexuality
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from listing real hardships with irrelevant personal detail

jokes.text: you have to be stubborn. But I ask you to put aside your stubbornness
jokes.setup: Homer praising Icelandic stubbornness
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking them to abandon that quality
jokes.explanation: Classic contradictory logic delivered without awareness

jokes.text: The blood of 1,000 tiny horses is on their hands.
jokes.setup: Crowd maintaining anger at Carl's family
jokes.punchline: Specific callback to earlier tiny horse reference
jokes.explanation: Effective callback to earlier culturally specific detail

jokes.text: that blue tape, you know, that makes you really look like you know what you're doing. You peel it off, and you got that super straight line there.
jokes.setup: Lenny describing Carl's helpfulness
jokes.punchline: Overly detailed appreciation of painter's tape
jokes.explanation: Lenny's earnest appreciation for mundane home improvement details

jokes.text: We don't get together to share our emotions. We get together to escape them.
jokes.setup: Carl learning about male friendship
jokes.punchline: Insightful observation about men's emotional avoidance
jokes.explanation: Profound and quotable insight about male friendship dynamics

jokes.text: Yeah, I'd tell you guys I love you, but, uh, I don't want to say it, and you don't want to hear it.
jokes.setup: Carl about to express emotions
jokes.punchline: Self-aware recognition of mutual emotional avoidance
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of male emotional communication patterns

jokes.text: Shh!
jokes.setup: End credits scene
jokes.punchline: Character shushing, likely visual gag
jokes.explanation: Minimal end credits gag, likely visual

tmdb_overview: Homer, Moe, Lenny and Carl win $200,000 in the Springfield Lottery. But when Carl takes off to Iceland – his homeland – with all the winnings, the guys embark on a Nordic adventure to get the cash back.
